entry.title: Introducing Joule on Hands-on Practice Sandbox
entry.content:
We are excited to announce to have Joule integrated into our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition sandbox.

Accessible with SAP Learning Hub subscription, sandbox systems help to explore, test, experiment, build use cases, and get a first look and feel of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition. Suitable for all roles and levels, it is filled with sample data that you can use to explore the functionality, experiment with scenarios, and test your results.

Joule helps to simplify your work

Joule, SAP’s generative AI copilot, revolutionizes how you interact with your SAP business systems, making every touchpoint count and every task simpler.

Joule is packed with powerful features that will transform how you engage with the practice system. Now you can benefit from Joule to:

  • Find and quickly navigate to applications for your next task.
  • Get fast insights on business data, such as purchase orders and outbound deliveries.
  • Receive help from user enablement content summarized in a crisp answer.

Joule

Watch this video to see Joule in action: >> GROW with SAP - Joule - The AI Copilot <<

Ready to get started?

We believe that learners thrive when they have the freedom to explore. That’s why we encourage you to experiment with Joule in our hands-on practice sandbox to see how it best fits your learning needs.

With a valid SAP Learning Hub subscription access the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition sandbox system and start testing.

Click the Joule icon at the top navigation and dive in:

Top Navigation

According to your assigned business role* start asking your questions in natural language, for example:

  • “What is a purchase order?”
  • “In which application can I see purchase orders?”
  • “Show me purchase order 4500000912”

*please refer to the system set-up guide about assigning of business roles to your user that comes with the Sandbox

System Set-Up Guide

 Also, check out the free “Exploring the SAP Business AI Portfolio" learning journey on the SAP Learning site to learn more about Joule and SAP Business AI.

 

You can find more information about sandbox systems here:

New Sandbox Available on the SAP Learning Site to ... - SAP Community

 

SAP News Article on Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: https://news.sap.com/?p=230358

 

Documentation on SAP Help Portal:  https://help.sap.com/docs/JOULE/82a14f108cfa4d4788244d81371e072b/a955cb79db3c4f5c89ed3f00bb05f080.html?locale=en-US&version=CLOUD

 

Note: To access Joule within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, an additional entitlement and authorization may be required. Please consult your account executive for more information.

 

Happy learning!

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Business Partner - Reuse of ECC custom field at S/4HANA with Clean Core principles
entry.content:
Introduction

A lot of customers have added custom fields to customer master. At system conversion from ECC to SAP S/4HANA you have to reimplement custom fields to BP transaction may based on blog post

SAP S/4HANA Business Partner - Field Enhancement

If you now want to reimplement this field in Master Data Fiori Apps you have to prepare this enhancement based on clean core principles.

SAP S/4HANA offers robust customization options, but maintaining a clean core is essential for system stability and simplified future upgrades. This guide covers adding custom database fields to standard Fiori apps while adhering to SAP's Clean Core principles.

In this blog post I will focus on fields of customer master data which maybe are already implemented at transaction BP.

Target audience: Functional expert, Development expert

 

Clean Core Principles

The Clean Core approach emphasizes minimal custom code and maximum use of standard SAP solutions, ensuring a stable and efficient system.

For more information on Clean Core principles, you can refer to following documents

ERP Clean Core Point-of-view

ERP clean core strategy

 

Preparation

Before adding custom fields, it’s crucial to ensure that your existing setup aligns with the Clean Core principles. Check out the blog post Convert Manually Created Append Fields to Real Key User Custom Fields for detailed steps. Additional information you can find at blog post Adding Custom database Field to standard Fiori apps in S/4HANA with Custom Fields and Logic

If field is implemented already in CVI and BP (SAP S/4HANA Business Partner - Field Enhancement)
Delete Z-field from CVI structure CVIS_EI_EXTERN as preparation (delete this from DATA and DATAX structures). It will be added later on with FIORI App "Custom Fields" enablement.

If field is not implemented yet or has been removed from structure CVIS_EI_EXTERN (field is only available at KNA1) you can proceed with following steps.

 

 

Start with ATO (Adaptation Transport Organizer) Setup

If you start transaction SCFD_EUI, you will get the following pop-up to set up ATO:

 

Step into ATO Setup for configuration:

If not done already, you can use a setup similar to this:

For ATO Setup, ensure the green traffic light appears at the top of the screen.

 

Check Activation Status

Run Transaction SCFD_EUI

Enter the database table and field.

Standard pattern for custom fields is setup with ZZ but original source of my example is an ECC system where custom fields start with Z. 

You will see your Z-field, which is marked as "Preparation Required."

 

Prepare the Field

Push the "Prepare" button to see the preparation details.

Click on one of the lines to see more detailed information about the ToDos:

Main Content in short: Move the Z-Field from the Z-Append-Structure to the standard enhancement structure INCL_EEW_KNA1, which is supported by the Clean Core approach.

 

Move Z-Field to Standard Enhancement Structure

To minimize data loss risk, activate both steps in one mass activation:

  1. Remove the field from the Append.
  2. Add the field to the Append in the Extension Include Structure.

Attention: Do not activate these two steps separately! Activating them separately may result in data loss.

Refer to SAP Note 3104595 - SCFD_EUI: Minimize risk for data loss when moving extension fields for more details on minimizing data loss risks:

 

Adjust Table in ABAP Dictionary

Run Transaction SE11:

Select table KNA1.

Here you can see your "old" Append Structure and the new target structure.

 

Create a New Append Structure

Assign your custom field from the "old" custom structure to the extensibility structure INCL_EEW_KNA1 by creating a new Append Structure at extensibility structure INCL_EEW_KNA1.

Save Changes

DO NOT activate.

 

Delete Custom Field from Old Append Structure

Save Changes

DO NOT activate them yet.

 

Mass Activation

Run Report RADMASG0_C3

Enter the new and old append structures into the Direct Object selection.

Run in Parallel as Background Job

Note: This process may take a long time (approximately 1.5 hours) due to the many dependencies of KNA1.

If you check the logs after activation, you may see errors. Ignore these for now; they will be corrected in the next step.

 

Delete Old Append Structure

After adding all custom fields to the new extensibility structure and activating them, you can delete the "old" append structure using transaction SE11.

This process will take some time. After activation, check if your data is still available in table KNA1 by running SE16N. Before that, check the table structure of KNA1.

 

Enable Field for Use in App "Custom Fields"

Check Activation Status Again

Transaction SCFD_EUI:

Enter the database table and field and confirm the notification.

As you can see, the custom field is now ready for activation and can be enabled.

You will get a proposal for the technical Fiori field. Note the difference between the technical database field (ZCUST) and the technical Fiori field (ZZ1_ZCUST).

Confirm the proposal pop-up. There will be additional pop-ups asking for development package and transport request details.

After enablement, you will see the following status:

Now your field is ready for the Fiori app "Custom Fields." With this enablement, the custom field has been added to CVI structure CVIS_EI_EXTERN automatically.

 

Enable field in Fiori App "Custom Fields"

Open Fiori App "Custom Fields"

As you can see, your new/"old" custom field is shown with the status "Published."

To check activation status click on the Field Name and Check Technical Object Activation. As an example, activate the field for the following technical objects to use in the Fiori app "Manage Customer Master."

For additional activation, it’s up to your requirements. A big advantage of this Fiori app is automatic enhancement of structures, IDOCs, OData Services, WebServices etc. by just clicking "Enable". Everything else is done automaicly in background.

 

Final Step: Extend Fiori Apps with Custom Fields

For more information on extending Fiori apps with custom fields, refer to the blog post: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Extend FIORI App "Manage Customer Master" with Z-field. Here you can skip the part Z-field definition and you can run Technical Object Activation directly.

 

Conclusion

Adding custom database fields to standard Fiori apps while maintaining a Clean Core in SAP S/4HANA involves several steps. By following these guidelines, you can ensure compliance, minimal custom code, and a stable system.

entry.title: Fiori Apps in Minutes using RAP framework/generator
entry.content:
This blog-tutorial will guide you through the process of creating a RAP application using ABAP repository objects generator.

Prerequisites:

SAP system access with eclipse installed. (You can use SAP BTP trial account as well)

Process:

  1. Create the required database table
    Right click on Package --> New --> Other ABAP Repository Object


    Search for database table and click on Next


    Provide name & description for the table and click on Next


    Create TR or select an existing one and click on Finish


    Add required fields to the table and activate it (you can also create a data element for user_id or can use any existing one)


  2. Generate objects for UI
    Right click on table name in project explorer and click on 'Generate ABAP Repository Objects'

     
    Select OData UI Service and click on Next


    Select Package and click on Next


    Provide names for all the artifacts/objects to be generated and click on Next


    Click on Next

    Select TR and finish



    You can see all the generated objects in the project explorer


  3. Publish service
    Open Service binding and click on Publish

    This option to publish an OData V4 service through eclipse might not work in a customizing client, for that you can refer to my other blog, i.e., Publishing ODATA v4 service in a customizing clien... - SAP Community

    Your basic RAP based Fiori application is ready for preview


  4. Preview Service
    Select entity set and click on Preview

    As this is a managed app, so few standard features like Create, Edit & Delete are handled by system.

    Try creating a new entry using Create button and the same will be visible on report page


    This can be checked on table level as well


    I will try to enhance this RAP application in future and include the same in my future blogs.

entry.title: Custom CDS Views – What’s New up to 2502
entry.content:
I haven't posted any updates on the Custom CDS Views app for a while now. It's not that the app does not evolve any more but the relation between developments under the hood versus features that are visible to you as a customer changes. Also the team is focussing on additional topics in the evolving area of developer extensibility.

So here is finally another update on the Custom CDS Views app including the recent features that will be shipped with 2502 as well as a selection of what has happened behind the scenes since 2308:

Manage & Convert Legacy CDS Views

As the old Custom CDS Views app is not available any more to manage the legacy views, we introduced additional options to cover the missing functionality in the new app. You can now not only migrate views to the new app, but also delete legacy views or unfinished drafts without the need to migrate them first (2308).Delete Legacy Views

The migration functionality was also enhanced to now migrate almost every view created with the old app to the new app (2502). If you now trigger the migration, the view structure will be transferred to the new app, afterwards, the view will be analysed and then re-published (2402). The analysis checks if the legacy view can automatically be adapted to the latest modelling rules. Re-publish will then update the view and modernise the structure (e.g. update to view entity, update the access protection or resolve rework tasks if possible).
Migrate Legacy ViewsIf the analysis finds issues that cannot be fixed automatically, the view will need to be adapted to the current rules manually. You will be supported by messages with instructions in the publishing log to change your view.

You may remember that we introduced scenarios with the new Custom CDS Views app. During the migration, the scenario is derived from various properties and from the usage of the CDS view. The only reason that may still prevent a migration is, if we cannot reliably derive the scenario. On the other hand this means that there is no usage of the view, otherwise we could have derived the scenario from the usage. In this case, you may want to consider to delete the unused view or to open a ticket (BC-SRV-APS-GKE) to get migration support.

Modernised Language Features

In 2023, Simple Types were introduced as a CDS language feature to include the possibility to define types natively in CDS. These data types can be used as well in ABAP as also in ABAP CDS. For more details, refer to the Simple Types blog post from Andrea.

As also SAP begins to use this language feature in their VDM data model, we started supporting simple types with 2408. This is specifically relevant when they are used in parameters.
This does not only help supporting more modern VDM content, but when you do use simple types in your developer extensibility CDS views (and C1 release them), you will profit from the option to use it in a Custom CDS View as well.

Rework Tasks

Rework tasks help you to keep track of maintenance tasks for your Custom CDS Views. Since the beginning of Key User Extensibility, we introduced different types of rework tasks as well in the old but also for the new Custom CDS Views app. With the old app becoming obsolete, we took the chance to modernise and consolidate our rework tasks.Rework Tasks

With 2308, we moved the rework calculation away from a health check to a background job. This makes the calculation more robust & reliable. With this move, we also introduced the rework tasks in the productive system that you can see the need for maintenance specifically for your productive views as well. In the production system, the rework tasks are calculated once a week over the week-end. A calculation directly after each import is planned as well.

As we now resolve some of the rework tasks that were specific to the old app during the migration, we could get rid of these rework tasks and replaced them with one task that just reminds you on the migration of legacy CDS views as such (2402).

The rework tasks around the new access protection were also optimised. These tasks do publish a complete view stack when it is necessary. In case of a large network of views, this sometimes leads to the need to transport a large amount of views even though only one view was changed. As of 2308 this publishing process was streamlined and improved. It is now ensured that you only need to transport the views that needed to be changed (specific to your development) which also improved the performance and reliability of the process.

Deprecated and Decommissioned Elements

The Virtual Data Model deprecated elements and replaced them with new ones for quite a while now. Still, they were not removed so far. Now VDM is going one step further and started decommissioning elements. The Custom CDS Views app reacts a bit differently to decommissioned elements now than it reacts to deprecated ones. In a nutshell, we do not allow including decommissioned elements to Custom CDS Views and whenever you transport a changed Custom CDS view, you have to remove the decommissioned elements before.Decommissioned Elements

To improve dealing with deprecated & decommissioned elements, we did the following changes in 2402:

  • The element value help shows an according Icon & Tooltip (also of associations)
  • We removed decommissioned elements from the elements value help
  • We raise a warning before deleting decommissioned elements (as you can't bring them back)
  • Decommissioned elements are marked in the elements tab

Usability Improvements

Whereas the raw data preview acts as a basic, built-in possibility to check the pure structure of the data, the preview via the separate Data Browser app was introduced as a more feature-rich alternative. Until 2408, it was only used to display the current Custom CDS View. Starting with 2402, it was integrated in the data sources tab as a possibility to preview other data sources (e.g. supported VDM views) as well.Preview Options

The selection of data sources was improved in 2308 already by adding the option to display and also to search and filter by components & packages. This allows you to identify and find related data sources more easily.Data Source Selection

What's next?

The custom CDS Views app is now quite mature. Language features like explicit joins or unions would unduly increase the complexity of the key user tool and therefore remain reserved for the developer extensibility.

We will therefore in future mainly invest in better usability of the app as well as in updating and modernising existing language features.

Apart from the key user world, you will hear from me about new tools for the developer extensibility - especially for Embedded Analytics. Stay tuned 🙂

entry.title: Service Management for Utilities in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Service Management with Advanced Execution in S/4HANA

To illustrate an utilities reference process flow, let me walk you through some screen shots, starting with a customer call and progressing to service and maintenance execution. I am including references to further reads. Then I talk about the main difference between S/4HANA Service including Service with Advanced Execution and the classic CS with comments about the required transition to S/4 Service.

 

Industry context

A utility providing both customer service and plant maintenance is either an integrated or a distribution company, responsible for maintaining the service connections from the grid to the customer’s premise and the meter. The industry specific service processes include device installations, off-cycle meter reads, disconnections and reconnections, outage reports or new service connection requests. Some of these processes are simple and short, they complete after a few hours, for example an offcyle meter read. Others like the service connection can take weeks or months until finished and settled. All these utilities service processes have in common that the end customer and the utility company equipment are part of the process flow, this is where customer service runs jointly with enterprise asset management.

 

Reference process flow

The S/4HANA capability for this is service with advanced execution. Service orders in this context are sometimes referred to as maintenance service orders (MSO).

During a renovation, a power connection or meter on the outside of a house was damaged. This equipment belongs to the utilities company so the customer decides to call. The utility company service agent looks up the address and creates a service request. It refers to the customer, the service location or equipment and usually has a category, priority, expected times and dates and notes from the caller.

Interaction Center in Customer Engagement for Utilities, service processes related to the connection object

The service request from the customer is routed to the service department responsible for energy or water distribution maintenance. In a deregulated market, this can cross company boundaries. A service manager creates a service order, because it is certain that a field service technician needs to investigate the damage and possibly exchange equipment. A service order with advanced execution is created as a follow-up document from the request. This type of service order has a service item with a service product like ‘METER EXCHANGE’.

Service order with advanced execution, header

Service order with advanced execution, item

Once released, this service order item creates a linked maintenance order. The maintenance order is responsible for the operational service delivery.

Service order, released, with linked maintenance order

If a back-office user expert user viewed the maintenance order transaction details, the reference to the service transaction is visible:

 

Service with Advanced Execution Overview

Now you have seen the key design in service with advanced execution, creating a service order that has a commercial or customer aspect with a service product that creates the maintenance order with the plant maintenance execution aspect.

 

 

Service with advanced execution is used with many process variations. The end-to-end process flow above shows several additional steps. For example, if the service request was for a new service connection, a service quote would be used. Once the quote is accepted by the customer or developer, the service order with advanced execution follows, as well as procurement like installation services by a 3rd party, goods movement like pipes or cables and billing to the customer.

 

 

Further reading on Service with Advanced Execution Overview

As an introduction, have a look at the SAP Help Portal

To help the implementation of utilities processes in S/4HANA Service, we have created a special cookbook

The released roadmap item for the utilities features from 2022 and contains demo videos in the ‘Features’ section SAP roadmap explorer 

The topic service with advanced execution is not specific to utilities, it is always helpful to check the cross-industry knowledge sources, for example here in the SAP community under the ERP section

Roadmap

I am excited about a Service Monitor Roadmap Item  in 2025, an app that will visualize the end-to-end process. This will help service experts to manage complex and long running processes like the new service connection in utilities.

 

S/4HANA Service compared to CS Service

Those who are using the PM/CS service objects today can see the key differences between ECC PM/CS or S/4HANA CS and S/4HANA Service. Below is a side by side process comparison of the classic CS service process on the left and the S/4HANA Service running jointly with S/4HANA Maintenance.

 

S/4HANA Service end of life for CS in 2030

S/4HANA Service is the go-to solution for any utility running the utilities metering and service processes. The customer service interaction center (Customer Engagement) contains only the (new) service processes. Other utilities processes including metering and service have been adapted to integrate the new service processes. For example an aperiodic meter reading now creates a service order with advanced execution.

Customers who are migrating from ECC CS to S/4HANA should switch to the S4 service processes at that time. Customers that are using CS today need to make plans to transition from CS to S/4HANA Service the latest by 2030. This is important, CS inside S/4HANA is also sunset, it is in a compatibility scope and the rights to use it end in 2030.

You can tell if you are running CS if you find PM/CS Notifications and PM/CS Service Orders. Make plans to retire them and switch over to the newer S/4HANA service objects including service request, service order with advanced execution and service quotation.

The timeline below shows how and when Service changed with Customer Management in S/4HANA.

More information in SAP Note 2269324.

 

 

Summary

The combination of complex customer and maintenance processes is an essential requirement for the energy and water distribution business. S/4HANA Service with advanced execution provides theses capabilities. Customer service initiates service requests, service quotes and service orders. The connected maintenance order is responsible for planning, scheduling, service execution and field service management.

S/4HANA Service with advanced execution is integrated with the utilities industry functions, including customer service and metering.

The classic PM/CS service objects notification and service orders reach their end of life in 2030, also within S/4HANA.

entry.title: Enable OData Streams in RAP
entry.content:
Problem statement:
How to enable OData Streams such as excel, pdf, Images and other large objects using standard RAP Based application development without using UI5 tooling?

Solution:

With the latest SAP BTP ABAP Environment 2208 release the RAP framework now supports OData streams. It is now possible to enable your RAP application to maintain and handle Large Objects(LOBs).This feature provides end users an option to upload external files of different file formats such as PDF, XLSX , binary file format and other types hence allowing media handling.

I am on of the presenter for this topic at ABAP Conference and int the below youtube recording. This topic covers content related to how to enable large objects using RAP (ABAP RESTful Programming Model ).  Upload functionality using custom actions, download functionality using custom actions,  how to use XCO library to read and write XLSX content and some useful links which can be explored about OData Streams in RAP.


ABAP Conference recording: Enable OData Streams in RAP

Slides: https://abapconf.org/abapconf2024/#/agenda

Check for topic "Enable OData Streams in RAP" where you can find the link to download slides in pdf document.

Use cases covered:

  1. Working with Large Objects
  2. Upload Functionality using Custom Action
  3. Download Functionality using Custom Action
  4. Use XCO library to read and write XLSX Content

Code based GitHub URL's:

1. Upload and Download Functionality application : https://github.com/rvrrpk/OdataStreamsRAP

2. Download History Application : https://github.com/rvrrpk/DownloadHistory

entry.title: Four Clean Core Use Cases for SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Business Technology Platform
entry.content:
Introduction

Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems have long been adapted to match companies' distinct business processes. However, as customizations accumulate, organizations are finding that maintaining their ERP functions becomes more complex, often slowing down innovation.  

In this short blog post, I’ll cover four use case examples with SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) you can adopt to simplify ERP upgrades with BTP extensions, while reducing testing time and effort. Customer examples are provided for each scenario, as well as links to technical implementation steps.

  1. Extend SAP S/4HANA processes without impacting core functionality
  2. Connect employees and partners to delight your customers
  3. Simplify process connectivity to any application
  4. Enhance SAP S/4HANA with planning and analysis

About the author

Terry Penner is part of the SAP BTP product marketing team, focused on BTP for SAP S/4HANA. He has more than 20 years of technical and business experience in the SAP platform and analytics, including working directly on hundreds of customer implementations.

Use case #1: Extend SAP S/4HANA processes without impacting core functionality

 

Innovative companies need to give their teams the tools to quickly turn great ideas into real-life changes. But for most businesses, it's still challenging to allow business users to create applications efficiently while maintaining IT governance standards.

Use SAP Build Apps so your SAP S/4HANA users can create extensions quickly without impacting core functionality.  Business users want to create specific workflows quickly without writing code, all while following IT company standards. For example, your team can design mobile and desktop applications and speed up development with prebuilt connectors and business content for SAP and third-party systems.

Freudenberg Group  adopted a low-code development strategy to 200 business users to help the company more quickly digitalize processes. This approach replaced manual paper document exchange, boosting new technology adoption fivefold. Now, the company is expanding this initiative to build a community inside Freudenberg for digital builder to solve key challenges and drive sustainable, value-driven growth.

>> Learn how to implement this use case

 

Use case #2: Connect employees and partners to delight your customers

The Lead to Cash process crosses multiple applications and connects employees and partners from initial interactions to order fulfillment. Real-time inventory levels and locations are vital, ensuring both sales reps and distribution partners have current data, especially when they may be competing for the same inventory.

Use pre-built integrations from SAP Integration Suite (part of SAP BTP) to connect SAP S/4HANA, finance and supply chain systems. As a result, your employees can boost business efficiency and provide a superior customer experience – all without requiring additional IT resources.

Vorwerk transformed the person-to-person sales experience by digitalizing the direct selling process. The world’s largest direct seller of premium household appliances used SAP Integration Suite and SAP BTP to connect back-end systems from SAP and third parties. This greatly reduced sales paperwork and significantly accelerated the sales cycle for their advisors.

>> Learn how to implement this use case

 

Use case #3: Simplify process connectivity to any application

Running a business on siloed legacy systems is no longer a competitive option.  A connected landscape is required to meet employee and customer expectations for simplified mobile access, omnichannel interactions, and collaborative engagement.

Open Connectors, a core component of SAP Integration Suite, offers over 160 prebuilt, feature-rich connectors to help ensure smooth connections with third-party SAP applications. The component helps create an integrated API layer and supports standards-based implementation for SAP and third-party applications, regardless of the back end. It facilitates critical digital capabilities, including mass-updating asset master data, generating purchase requisitions originating from a spreadsheet, and uploading manual entries automatically through an API.

Elkjøp is a prime example of a company leveraging this use case to build integrations with third-party applications. Using SAP Integration Suite, the consumer electronics retailer created an event driven architecture to connect services, repairs, installations, capacity handling and support tickets. The company processes 5 million messages daily to provide an experience where a customer can place an online order and pick it up in-store within 30 minutes.

>> Learn how to implement this use case

 

Use case #4: Enhance SAP S/4HANA with planning and analysis

Effective use of financial data safeguards your business from damaging risks, such as maverick spending, regulatory noncompliance, and reporting errors. But it also optimizes a finance organization's already limited time and energy.

By enabling native connectivity to SAP S/4HANA Cloud data from SAP Analytics Cloud, you can create a fully integrated foundation for aligning your operations and finance strategies. Business leaders can use financial planning and analysis to gain a real-time, business-wide understanding of critical business drivers to mitigate potential risks early, address planning variances proactively, and improve profitability. Company specific financial planning can be accomplished while still allowing for easier updating of SAP S/4HANA core modules.

Take Watco, a leading transportation and logistics provider in the United States. Watco brought together data from more than 200 different profit centers to provide a consolidated company view of financials. This allows the company to analyze different data sets to provide improved forecasting and a better link between financial planning and ERP. 

>> Learn how to implement this use case

 

Innovation without compromising ERP integrity

With SAP BTP together with SAP S/4HANA, it is possible to have the ERP experience that meets your business needs today, tomorrow, and in the future.  By applying these four use cases, you can help ensure the ERP system operates efficiently and supports the innovation of digital capabilities unique to your business. You can design custom applications, create connected experiences, simplify connectivity, and align operations and finances – all without compromising the integrity of your ERP investment.

For more use cases that can unleash your company’s potential, learn more about SAP Business Technology Platform with SAP S/4HANA.

 

Explore more related content in this series about SAP BTP with S/4HANA:

Overview

  • 2024 SAP BTP Year in Review: Release Highlights for SAP S/4HANA users

Artificial Intelligence

  • Artificial Intelligence (AI) for SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP: A deep dive
  • How SAP Business AI and Large Language Models are shaping the future of SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP 

Data Migration

  • SAP S/4HANA Data Migration and Master Data Management Best Practices with SAP BTP

Extensions

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensions with SAP Build Best Practices: An Expert Roundtable
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensions and Clean Core with SAP BTP - A Best Practices Conversation

entry.title: Activity-Related Cost Planning in SAP S/4 HANA
entry.content:
This post is related to SAP S/4 HANA (Public and Private Cloud Edition) and explains how to prepare data for activity-related cost planning.

The data can then be imported into table ACDOCP via the Fiori app Import Financial Plan Data. This post is also relevant for other methods of writing to ACDOCP, such as writeback from SAP Analytics Cloud (in case of not using the SAP-delivered content in SAP Analytics Cloud called Integrated Financial Planning that is already modelled according to these requirements) or the write API that is available in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

The data structure is illustrated based on two examples. The first one introduces direct activity allocation, the second one deals with the valuation of activity-related services whose costs are split into a fixed and variable amount.

Contrary to cost center planning in SAP ECC (SAP ERP Central Component), where activity types and associated costs are planned in separate steps, and then the activity price is determined, this approach describes how the result of activity-related planning (with valuated activities) can be depicted properly in SAP S/4 HANA.

In SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Private Edition, the activity-related costs in ACDOCP can be transferred to SAP ECC tables using the provided transfer program (e.g. for the justification of activity prices), so it can be displayed in the tables COSS and COSL and used in transactions KP26 (for activity output planning) and KP06 (for activity input planning).

If the cost rates used for the valuation of planned activity consumption is required in SAP S/4 HANA (e.g. for product cost calculation or the valuation of actual activity consumption), there’s a difference between SAP S/4 HANA with activated business function Universal Parallel Accounting (FINS_PARALLEL_ACCOUNTING_BF) and without. This business function is active by default in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and can be activated in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition.

The business function offers the SAP Fiori app Manage Cost Rates – Plan for maintaining cost rates. If the business function is not activated, use transaction KP26 (Edit Prices for Activity Types - Cost Centers).

Plan data for activity-related costs appears as usual in the reporting apps for financial plan data, as well as in cost center-specific plan/actual comparisons.

The following principles in the universal journal also apply to activity and cost allocation planning between two cost objects, so stick to them when preparing the data for the import:

  • Activity output with the cost center providing activities (database field “RCNTR”) to partners (database field “SCNTR”) is represented as credit records (debit/credit indicator ‘C’) and corresponding negative amounts.
  • In contrast, activity input with the cost center consuming activities (database field “RCNTR”) from partners (database field “SCNTR”) is represented as debit records (debit/credit indicator ‘D’) and corresponding positive amounts.
  • Therefore to display an activity consumed by the sender (as activity output) and receiver (activity input), the debit/credit indicator and amount sign must be swapped, as explained above, as well as the associated object and partner object (e.g. receiving and sending cost center).
  • Planned activity output (with activity-related costs) can be credited either to a partner cost center (as consumer) or as a total amount without a partner cost center; a combination of both is possible as well.
  • To differentiate between fixed and variable activity quantity and costs, the measures for total and fixed quantity as well as total and fixed amount are used. Details on the calculation of the total and variable portions are explained below.

These principles are demonstrated using the following examples. In both examples, cost centers serve as cost objects; other cost objects like projects or production orders are not discussed here.

Example 1: Introduction to Direct Internal Activity Allocation in ACDOCP

In this example, a maintenance cost center MAINTAIN is responsible for periodic maintenance and case-based repairs, which are categorized as activity types MAINT and REPAIR, and both are measured in hours (h). Two manufacturing cost centers, MANUFACT01 and MANUFACT02, consume services from the maintenance cost center. All cost centers are in the same company code.

For illustration it is assumed that besides the activity-receiving cost centers MANUFACT01 and MANUFACT02 (which consume a portion from cost center MAINTAIN), cost center MAINTAIN plans an additional activity output without defining any receiving cost centers (700 hours activity type MAINT, 75 hours activity type REPAIR). However, in order to keep the plan aligned, in practice this planning begins once the receiving cost centers have completed their activity consumption plan before cost centers providing services start their activity output plan.

This example illustrates how activity output and input is represented in the universal journal. Furthermore it shows that activity output can be credited either to a partner cost center (as consumer) or as an amount that represents the total output with unspecified partner cost centers. Finally, the sum of all credited output quantity corresponds to the total of all services provided to the respective cost center.

For simplicity, the calculation of the value of the supplied services assumes that the underlying cost rates are not divided into fixed and variable amounts (see second example for an activity cost rate split into a fixed and variable amount).

Here is an overview:

The following table gives an overview of the calculated values ​​based on the standard formula price (P) times quantity (M):

The previously described principles and calculated values can be seen in the following data in table ACDOCP:

As explained before, discharges (activity output) are depicted as credit records with the supplier cost center in the database field “RCNTR” and associated negative amounts and quantities (records 2, 4, 6, 8). In contrast, charges (activity input) are shown as debits with the consuming cost center in the database field “RCNTR” and with associated positive amounts and quantities.

The above data also shows that a combination is possible where the delivery quantity is booked to partner cost centers (see record 2, 4, 6, 8 ) and any remaining delivery quantity to unspecified partners is booked as a total amount (see records 9 & 10).

Example 2: Value calculation of provided and consumed services

This example is now supplemented with a cost calculation of provided and consumed activities that is based on an activity cost rate split into a fixed and variable amount. In general, fixed costs are independent of services provided, whereas variable costs are not.

Furthermore the example shows that costs which were variable on the sender side, can become fixed costs on the receiving side.

In this example, the cost center POWERPLANT provides electricity as activity type ELCTRCTY, which is consumed by the two manufacturing cost centers MANUFACT01 and MANUFACT02 and thus ensures planned machine running times of 7,600 and 6,300 hours, respectively.

For illustration we also assume that at cost center MANUFACT02 the consumption of activity type ELCTRCTY is 100% variable, depending on its driving quantity MCHHRS2, whereas at cost center MANUFACT01 a portion of the consumption of activity type ELCTRCTY depends on its driving quantity MCHHRS1 and the remaining consumption is fixed (because the machines in cost center MANUFACT01 cannot be shut down on short notice). For the supplying cost center, after determination of the activity price (on a full-cost basis), a transfer price is calculated that is partly fixed, and the rest is variable.

This example is shown in the following figure:

The formulas for the different calculations are listed below:

Based on the example of cost center MANUFACT01, for the 45,000 kwh, a fixed valuation is done. As a result of this cost allocation for cost center MANUFACT01 (receiver), the fixed share of the allocated amount is higher than for cost center POWERPLANT (sender).

In contrast, for the fixed costs to be charged to cost center MANUFACT02, the total consumed quantity of 94,500 kWh is valued at the fixed price of the cost rate for activity ELCTRCTY, so that in this case the fixed valuation of the supplying cost center POWERPLANT is used.

The following ACDOCP representation is similar to Example 1, with the difference that it only includes one activity type that is again consumed by two cost centers. For the cost rate split into fixed and variable, fixed amounts and quantities are shown separately (KFSL, VMFSL).

Import file preparation for SAP Fiori app “Import Financial Plan Data

The examples above show that for partner relations between cost objects like cost centers, the sending cost center and partner activity are necessary. The records are formed with the debit/credit indicator and the amount sign (+/-). This concept must also be used in the import file. Since the debit/credit indicator is set to a default value ‘D’, it must be added as a column to the import file with the corresponding values.

To display a split amount and quantity in the universal journal, a total value and a fixed value must be provided, so that the variable value can be calculated by subtracting the fixed value from the total value. Consequently, the two columns KSL and KFSL are used for the global currency type and the two columns VMSL and VMFSL are used for the valuation quantities as shown in the second import file example.

Both examples are based on the global currency type for loading the data for the examples shown above. Posting period ‘1’ was assumed in both files, and in both examples, the delete scope is set up so that each file can only be uploaded entirely to avoid records overwriting each other.

Import File 1

Import File 2

entry.title: Trade Compliance and Customs Process with SAP Transportation Management for Shippers in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Global Trade is a very vast topic. With this SAP blog post I want to narrow down and focuses on very specific use case which is of a Shipper using SAP Transportation Management system to ship goods internationally. Shipper may use foreign-trade compliant SAP Global Trade Services (SAP GTS) solution or any other non-SAP solution.

Standard product documentation:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_GLOBAL_TRADE_SERVICES_EDITION_HANA/3e27235faf0442d380b529b89bb82982/4a9eb3af0c5b388fe10000000a42189c.html?locale=en-US

Compliance Management Control: Compliance Management helps ensure your compliance with international trade regulations. Compliance Management helps you rationalize your extended logistics chain and automate the complicated processes that are involved with international trade compliance issues, a primary prerequisite for successful international trading activities. This minimizes the risk of having to pay penalties and fines. 

  1. Sanction Party List Screening check: Sanctioned Party involved in the Transportation (Shipper, Consignee, Carrier etc.). A sanctioned party list contains a list of persons and companies with whom trade is prohibited by law. Sanctioned party lists are issued by government agencies and can be obtained from data providers. Sanctioned party lists are binding for all traders. During sanctioned party list screening, you compare your business partner addresses with the addresses on the relevant sanctioned party lists. When you export goods, for example, you can automatically compare the consignees' addresses with the sanctioned party list.
  2. Legal Control check: Legal control includes checking business transactions for products that require licenses, as well as the check for embargo situations for these business transactions.
    • Managing Licenses: The authorities define bans and restrictions for individual products or product groups to control their import and/or export. To import and export these products despite such restrictions, you can apply for licenses from the authorities. These licenses restrict goods traffic, for example, through quantity quotas or maximum values for goods. The authorities define license requirements for products based on import/export control classification numbers, with which you classify your products. Based on this classification, the system can detect which document items in a business transaction require licenses.
    • Embargo Checks: The government agencies of a country, group of countries, or international organizations as representatives of the United Nations sometimes impose bans on shipping specific products to specific countries, for business or political reasons, known as embargos. You have to comply with these embargos in your import and export processes.

Customs Management Control: The system helps your company automate and standardize import and export processes with the customs authorities. This enables you to use the import and export of goods faster and reduce the total costs for handling customs-relevant transactions. One essential prerequisite for this is electronic communication with the customs authorities in the individual countries that use computerized procedures. Electronic customs processing with the application helps companies meet the stringent requirements of the legal regulations, accelerating the release of their goods by the customs authorities.

Relevant information like shipment route, packaged cargo details, involved countries, Customs Invoice etc. is needed to file the Export/Import Declaration and receive a confirmation and Customs Reference Numbers.

From a point of view of SAP Transportation Management solution the Trade Compliance and Customs check functionality is embedded in the transport documents. APIs in the form of Service interfaces are provided from SAP TM to integrate SAP Global Trade Services (SAP GTS)

For Trade Compliance Check:

  • TradeComplianceCheckSUITERequest_Out
  • TradeComplianceCheckSUITEConfirmation_In

Further information on setup and process can be found in the product help

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/e3dc5400c1cc41d1bc0ae0e7fd9aa5a2/973af566a5eb481da5161fcc7f118ca9.html?locale=en-US

For Customs process:

  • ExportDeclarationSUITERequest_Out
  • ExportDeclarationSUITEConfirmation_In

Further information on setup and process can be found in the product help

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/e3dc5400c1cc41d1bc0ae0e7fd9aa5a2/9eaf60c26c9b4067acddf492d8aa9376.html?locale=en-US

Freight Unit being the representation of Cargo being transported is near to the Compliance Management function with clear status for Embargo, Legal Control and Sanctioned Party Screening, for each material shipped. Checks are performed during creation and any relevant change to the freight unit data to always stay compliant. Also, once the “shipment” or what we call as a Freight Order is created with additional logistical information and Carrier involved, compliance check is also performed to stay compliant before the start of execution.

Customs Management (receiving export declaration confirmation etc.) is more on the “shipment” level or Freight Order Level. Before filing for customs declaration certain information like shipment route, packaged cargo, involved countries, Customs Invoice etc. is needed. Most of this information is already available in the Freight Order, customs invoice can be requested from the Freight Order.

Specific Customs and Compliance status are made visible in the Freight Order and execution blocks are enforced until the shipment has all the necessary required approvals in place.

Happy reading !!!

entry.title: Track and Trace KITS in the field using S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Pre-requisite:

  • Hospitals must be set up as customers and sales representatives as employees
  • Instruments and Consumables set up separate materials
  • Sales BOM creation to group instruments
  • Install base configuration
  1. Create a KIT in SAP
 

Step 1: Retrieve IBASE number in the front-end field management system. To create a KIT the front-end system will call SAP and create an IBASE. IBASE number would then be communicated back to the source system

Ste2: Front end management system will then use the IBASE number to construct a KIT in the field and relevant items

Step 3: Once the KIT build is complete front-end system will communicate the details to the SAP

Step 4: SAP adds the materials to the IBASE and kicks off the program to create an asset

  1. Add or delete items in a KIT

Instrument KITs can be upgraded by adding new items in the field, below are the steps to accomplish this process

Step 1: Identify KIT and the component to be added. KITs are identified using SAP IBASE number

Step 2: Add the component to the IBASE in the front-end system and communicate the addition to S4

Step 3: S4 Add items to the existing IBASE and create a sub-asset for the added item

 

Conclusion:

  • After going through the above blog, the user should have learned how to fulfill trunk stock requests from medical sales representatives using S4 HANA

 

  • Please provide your feedback/comments. You can ask Question / Answer for the open Questions in the SAP Community here

Reference:

 SAP Help Portal

Authors: Mohan Kaliyamoorthy and Immanuel Godwin Johnson

entry.title: Surgery case fulfillment and replenishment
entry.content:
Pre-requisite:

  • Hospitals must be set up as customers and sales representatives as employees
  • Instruments and Consumables set up separate materials
  • Sales BOM creation to group instruments

Step 1: Define Consignment fill up, pick up, and Issue document types, which is the return delivery of goods to an external vendor.

SPRO – Sales and Distribution – Sales document- Sales document header – Define sales document type.

Consignment Fill up. – To deliver medical instruments for the surgery

Consignment Pick up – To return medical instrument after surgery

Consignment Issue – Bill for the implants consumed in the surgery.

Step 2: Define item categories.

SPRO – Sales and Distribution – Sales document- Sales document item – Define item categories

Consignment Fill-up Item category.

Consignment pick-up item category

Consignment Issue item category

Step 3: Schedule lines

SPRO – Sales and Distribution – Sales document- Schedule lines – Define schedule lines.

Consignment Fill up the schedule line

Consignment Pickup Schedule line.

Consignment Issue schedule line

Step 4: Assign document type to the sales area.

SPRO – Sales and Distribution – Sales document- Sales document header – Assign Sales Area to Sales Document Types

Step 5: Assign Item Categories

SPRO – Sales and Distribution – Sales document- Sales document item - Assign Item Categories

Step 6: Assign Schedule Line Categories

SPRO – Sales and Distribution – Sales document- Schedule lines - Assign Schedule Line Categories

Process steps: -

  1. Create a Consignment fill-up (KB) order for a surgery event for the requested instrumentation loaner kits and implants/disposables.

     

  2. Step 2: Create an outbound delivery with reference to the KB Order. Pick, pack, and Issue

    Step 3: Bill the customer for the materials consumed during the surgery using the Consignment issue (KE)

    Step 4: Create delivery and issue from consignment stock

    Step 5: Invoice the customer for the amount consumed.

    Step 8: Create a Consignment fill-up (KB) order for replenishment implants/disposables consumed in the surgery

    Step 9: Create an outbound delivery with reference to the KB Order. Pick, pack, and Issue

    Conclusion:

    • After going through the above blog, the user should have learned how to fulfill, pick up, bill, and replenish medical devices/instruments for a Surgery event in a hospital using S4 HANA
    • Please provide your feedback/comments. You can ask Question / Answer for the open Questions in the SAP Community here

     

    Reference:

    S/4 HANA Consignment Process:

    Consignment Stock Processing | SAP Help Portal

    Authors: Mohan Kaliyamoorthy and Immanuel Godwin Johnson

entry.title: Transfer of KITS from one Branch to another branch using S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Pre-requisite:

  • The goods requested for transfer are consigned in the system against branch A
  • Virtual storage location set up in S4 to move stock between the rep and not visible to ATP
  • Hospitals and branches must be set up as customers
  • Instruments and Consumables set up separate materials.

 

Setup Steps:

Configure new consignment document type: -

Step 1: Define Consignment fill up and pick up document types

SPRO – Sales and Distribution – Sales document- Sales document header – Define sales document type.

Consignment Pick up – To pick up inventory from branch A back to the warehouse and virtual storage location

Consignment Fill up. – To deliver inventory to sales rep B

Step 2: Define item categories.

SPRO – Sales and Distribution – Sales document- Sales document item – Define item categories

Consignment pick-up item category

Consignment Fill-up Item category ( KBN)


Step 3: Schedule lines

SPRO – Sales and Distribution – Sales document- Schedule lines – Define schedule lines.

Consignment Pickup Schedule line.

Consignment Fill up the schedule line

Step 4: Assign document type to the sales area.

SPRO – Sales and Distribution – Sales document- Sales document header – Assign Sales Area to Sales Document Types

Step 5: Assign Item Categories

SPRO – Sales and Distribution – Sales document- Sales document item - Assign Item Categories

Step 6: Assign Schedule Line Categories

SPRO – Sales and Distribution – Sales document- Schedule lines - Assign Schedule Line Categories

Process steps: -

Step 1: - Create pick up order (KA) To pick up inventory from branch A

Step 2: Create Inbound delivery and receive goods in the warehouse of origin and virtual storage location.

Step 3. Create a Consignment fill up (KB) order transfer/deliver goods to branch B

Step 4: Create an outbound delivery with reference to the KB Order. Pick, pack, and Issue from the virtual storage location to rep B

Step 5: Asset updated with the new functional location


Conclusion:

  • After going through the above blog, the user should have learned how to transfer goods from one branch to another using S4 HANA
  • Please provide your feedback/comments. You can ask Question / Answer for the open Questions in the SAP Community here

Reference:

S/4 HANA Consignment Process:

Consignment Stock Processing | SAP Help Portal

Authors: Mohan Kaliyamoorthy and Immanuel Godwin Johnson

entry.title: Jumpstart your SAP RISE Project with SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
For a SAP RISE project, SAP Cloud ALM offers many accelerators that you can use out of the box to set up your project. In this blog post I would like to describe how to get started.

1. Create a Project by using SAP Activate Roadmap

  • Choose the SAP Activate Roadmap "RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - Implementation"

2. Create a Scope and choose the Solution Scenarios

  • Create a Scope and choose the relevant Solution Scenarios

3. Review the proposed roadmap tasks

  • Check the standard tasks, create additional ones or delete unnecessary ones

4. Review the Quality Gates

  • Check the predefined Quality Gates and schedule them for the right timebox
  • Create additional Quality Qates with Checklists

5. Additional functionalities

Use other desired functions such as Requirements Management, Test Management, Release Management, etc.

entry.title: An Introduction to Documentation Versions on the SAP Help Portal for Functional Localization
entry.content:
I’m pleased to share the latest information on accessing documentation on the SAP Help Portal for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. In this blog post, I’ll explain how you can choose the right documentation version for your technical requirements and discuss the recent updates on the SAP Help Portal for maintenance phases.

Title image for the topic of documentation versions on the SAP Help Portal

I’ll be focusing today on the country/region-specific documentation for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition that is published through the Documentation Delivery with SAP Notes process, though there are useful tips here for everyone who browses version-specific content on the SAP Help Portal.

1. Choosing the Right Documentation Version for You

The SAP Help Portal is your number-one resource for information on SAP products. When navigating documentation, it’s important to verify that the version you’re browsing is correct for your solution. You can do this by checking the Version Selector drop-down menu, as shown in this example from the Local Versions page:

Drop-down version selection on the SAP Help Portal

Why do we recommend this version verification step? Well, you can access SAP Help Portal documentation by many different means, such as links from SAP Notes and What’s New topics, as well as from search engines. You may be directed to the highest version, which is not necessarily the same version implemented by your company. Different versions may have different scope functionality described in the documentation, so it’s best to always check this drop-down menu when accessing the SAP Help Portal.

2. New on the SAP Help Portal: Maintenance Information

You might have noticed that, when you access documentation for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, there’s a new information banner that specifies the maintenance phase:

Maintenance message now available on the SAP Help Portal

This information banner differentiates between two maintenance phases, Mainstream Maintenance and Extended Maintenance. If you are an SAP customer or partner with a valid SAP S-User ID, you can refer to the SAP Maintenance Phases resource for further details on this topic.

For the most recent documentation versions on the SAP Help Portal, you are informed that “documentation is updated with the support package stacks”. However, there is an important exception to this rule for country/region-specific updates delivered for functional localization.

3. Always Up-to-Date: Documentation Delivery for Functional Localization

For country/region-specific development delivered for functional localization, such as legal changes, a new process provides documentation updates between support package stacks. As detailed in the recent blog post on Documentation Delivery on the SAP Help Portal with SAP Notes, you now have access to SAP Help Portal documentation that reflects the latest information, regardless of the planned support package release dates.  

For both the mainstream and extended maintenance phases of SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, we’re glad to provide you with up-to-date information on how SAP meets the local legal requirement for your solution. 

To find more information on country/region-specific topics, I recommend the Localization topic page on the SAP Community. There you can find the latest news, resources, and upcoming events for SAP’s localized solutions. Remember, too, that you can always select the Create a Question option on the Localization topic page to engage with experts and resolve any doubts you may have:

Create a Question for Localization on the SAP Community

And for those of you who use SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, you can find more details on the SAP Help Portal and SAP Community in our dedicated blog series:

  • An Introduction to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Navigating the SAP Help Portal
  • An Introduction to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Engaging with the SAP Community

That’s it for today, though please let me know in the comments section if you have any questions. Your feedback is always appreciated.

Thank you and until next time,

Barnabas

entry.title: Performing AMDP CURD OPERATIONS
entry.content:
AMDP CURD OPERATIONS 

 AMDP CURD Operations: 

AMDP (ABAP Managed Database Procedures) is a feature in SAP HANA that allows you to write database procedures in SQL Script directly within the ABAP environment. AMDP provides a seamless way to execute database-level operations from ABAP while leveraging the power of HANA's in-memory capabilities. When it comes to CRUD operations (Create, Read, Update, Delete), here's how they are typically handled with AMDP: 

Why Use AMDP for CRUD Operations? 

Performance: AMDPs run directly on the database layer, making them faster than traditional ABAP for complex operations. 

Complex Scenarios: 
AMDP can handle bulk data operations efficiently, such as processing thousands of orders in real time. 

Seamless Integration: 

AMDP allows developers to stay in the ABAP environment while leveraging HANA’s advanced database features like parallel processing. 

Business Scenarios: 

A manufacturing company wants to streamline its sales process to ensure accurate order fulfillment and improve customer satisfaction. The company uses AMDP in the SD module to handle the following tasks: 

  1. Sales Order Creation: Automatically reserve stock or trigger purchase requisitions if stock is insufficient. 
  1. Order Status Tracking: Provide real-time updates on sales order status. 
  1. Delivery Management: Update the status of deliveries once completed. 

 Steps to achieve AMDP CURD Operations: 

Create a AMDP class.

Most important part is here is, if you want to use any class with AMDP’s, you have to implement “ if_amdp_marker_hdb” interface. 

So when you are writing the method of the class you have to add some keywords with which tables you are going to use, if not you cannot do any operations on these tables. 

So we add both method declarations and table names to our methods. 

Create operation: 

 

METHOD create_data BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT USING zgr_t_booking. 

it_insert_data = SELECT 'BID0000103', 'RUSHITHA', 'KADAPA', 'BANGALORE', 100, ' ' frOM dummy ; 
inSERT iNTO zgr_t_booking seLECT * FROM :it_insert_data ; 

ENDMETHOD.

 

Output: 

Update Operation: 

 

METHOD update_data BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT USING zgr_t_booking.

UPDATE zgr_t_booking SET passenger_name = 'RESHMA' WHERE booking_id = 'BID0000103' ; 

ENDMETHOD.

 

Output: 

Read Operations: 

 

METHOD read_data BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT USING zgr_t_booking. 

it_booking = SELECT * FROM zgr_t_booking ; 

ENDMETHOD.

 

Output: 

Delete Operation: 

 

METHOD delete_data BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT USING zgr_t_booking. 

DELETE fROM zgr_t_booking WHERE booking_id = 'BID0000101' ; 

ENDMETHOD.

 

Output: 

AMDP CURD operation Entire Logic :

 

CLASS zcl_gr_amdp_curd DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.

    TYPES: tt_booking TYPE TABLE OF zgr_t_booking.

    INTERFACES if_amdp_marker_hdb.
    CLASS-METHODS : create_data,
      update_data,
      read_data EXPORTING VALUE(it_booking) TYPE tt_booking,
      delete_data.
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_gr_amdp_curd IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD create_data BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT USING zgr_t_booking.

    it_insert_data = SELECT 'BID0000103', 'RUSHITHA', 'KADAPA', 'BANGALORE', 100,'' frOM dummy ;

    inSERT iNTO zgr_t_booking seLECT * FROM :it_insert_data ;

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD update_data BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT USING zgr_t_booking.

    UPDATE zgr_t_booking SET passenger_name = 'RESHMA' WHERE booking_id = 'BID0000103' ;

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD read_data BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT USING zgr_t_booking.

    it_booking = SELECT * FROM zgr_t_booking ;

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD delete_data BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT USING zgr_t_booking.

    DELETE fROM zgr_t_booking WHERE booking_id = 'BID0000101' ;

  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

entry.title: Method Chaining: A Modern Approach to Simplifying SAP ABAP Code
entry.content:
What Is Method Chaining?  

Method Chaining in ABAP allows you to call multiple methods one after the other in a single statement, without needing helper variables. This makes your code cleaner and easier to read. 

Previously, ABAP only supported chaining of attributes in class calls. But starting with ABAP 7.0 EhP2, you can also chain methods along with attributes.  

 Key Features of Method Chaining in SAP ABAP 

  1. Chained Execution: Allows multiple methods to be executed in a single statement. 
  2. Fluent Interface Design: Methods return the same object (SELF), enabling seamless chaining of operations. 
  3. Enhanced Readability: Eliminates the need for intermediate variables, making the code cleaner and more intuitive. 
  4. Concise Code: Reduces the number of lines of code, improving maintainability. 
  5. Modern ABAP Practice: Aligns with modern programming paradigms and best practices.
  6. Ease of Debugging: Individual methods can be tested independently while supporting chaining.

Advantages of Method Chaining 

Improved Readability: Code is more concise and easier to understand. 

Fewer Variables: Reduces the need for intermediate variables.

Aligned with OOP: Encourages a clean and modern ABAP development style. 

Reusable Code: Enables building reusable and modular classes.

Fluent Interface: Helps developers create a more intuitive and fluent API for their classes. 

Example for Method Chaining: 

I have taken 3 classes and one method in each class and given implementations for all three methods of a Class. 

 

 

REPORT zch_rp_method_chaining.
CLASS lcl_discount DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS apply_discount IMPORTING im_price                   TYPE i
                                     im_discount                TYPE i
                           RETURNING VALUE(re_discounted_price) TYPE i.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS lcl_tax DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS apply_tax IMPORTING im_price                 TYPE i
                                im_tax_rate              TYPE i
                      RETURNING VALUE(re_price_with_tax) TYPE i.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS lcl_final_price DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS calculate_final_price IMPORTING im_price              TYPE i
                                  RETURNING VALUE(re_final_price) TYPE i.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS lcl_discount IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD apply_discount.
    re_discounted_price = im_price - ( im_price * im_discount / 100 ).
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS lcl_tax IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD apply_tax.
    re_price_with_tax = im_price + ( im_price * im_tax_rate / 100 ).
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS lcl_final_price IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD calculate_final_price.
    re_final_price = im_price.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

 

 

Without Method Chaining if we want to call the method :

We need to create Object reference for each class and using that reference we need to call the method.   

apply_discount Method: Accepts two parameters and returns a result.  

apply_tax Method: Takes the result of  apply_discount method as input for im_price .  

calculate_final_price : Uses the result of apply_tax as input for im_price and returns the final output

 

 

START-OF-SELECTION.
data : lo_discount type REF TO lcl_discount,
      lo_tax type ref to lcl_tax,
      lo_final_price type ref to lcl_final_price.

data : lv_discounted_price type i,
      lv_price_with_tax type i,
      lv_final_price type i.

CREATE OBJECT : lo_discount,
                lo_tax,
                lo_final_price.

"without method chaining
lv_discounted_price = lo_discount->apply_discount(
                        im_price    = 500
                        im_discount = 10
                      ).
lv_price_with_tax = lo_tax->apply_tax(
                      im_price    = lv_discounted_price
                      im_tax_rate = 5
                    ).
lv_final_price = lo_final_price->calculate_final_price( im_price =  lv_price_with_tax ).

write :/'Final price without method chaining ',lv_final_price.

 

With Method Chaining 

 

"With method chaining
lv_final_price = lo_final_price->calculate_final_price(
                  EXPORTING im_price = lo_tax->apply_tax(
                  EXPORTING im_price = lo_discount->apply_discount(
                                                    im_price = 500
                                                    im_discount = 10 ) im_tax_rate = 5 ) ).

write  'Final price with method chaining ',lv_final_price.

 

 

We can notice in the above example, 3 lines of code and 3 helper variables are converted to 1 method chain and 1 return variable. 

Method Chaining Structure: 

  • Innermost Call (apply_discount): 
    Calculates the discounted price: 500 - (500 * 10 / 100) = 450. 
  • Middle Call (apply_tax): 
    Applies tax to the discounted price: 450 + (450 * 5 / 100) = 473. 
  • Outermost Call (calculate_final_price): 
    Returns the final calculated price: 473. 

 Important Points

  1. For Method chaining, the system would start processing from the Inner Most method call to outer most method. 
  2. Chained Method Call Requires Functional Methods i.e they must have a RETURNING parameter .
    The Accepting Method Should Have Importing Parameters , If there are multiple importing parameters, they should be optional so the system can decide which parameter to use when chaining or need to use the EXPORTING keyword to make parameter assignments clear.
  3. The RETURNING parameter from one method must match the type of the importing parameter in the next method.
    If the types are different but compatible, the system will automatically     convert the value. 
    1. In dynamic method calls, parameter handling is slower because the system dynamically determines the method at runtime. 
      Ex: CALL METHOD lo_obj->(method_name) 
                EXPORTING  
                   im_price = 1000 
                   im_discount = 10 
              IMPORTING  
                  re_discounted_price = lv_final_price.         
      SAP Recommendation: Use helper variables to store intermediate results instead of chaining methods dynamically. 
      Ex: DATA: lv_discounted_price TYPE i. 
      lv_discounted_price = lo_sales->apply_discount(  
                              im_price = 1000  
                              im_discount = 10 ). 

  Debugging: 

In Debugging we have a tab called Auto in variable display tool. This tab would show you the step details whenever a method chain would be executed 

When you execute the statement using F6, you would notice 3 steps entry created in the Auto tab.
We can see here, system has started processing from the Inner Most method call to outer most    method. 

 

Conclusion 
Method chaining in ABAP is a powerful technique for writing clean and modern code. It enhances readability and aligns with best practices in object-oriented programming. Whether you're building complex queries, configuring objects, or designing APIs, method chaining can simplify your ABAP code and improve its maintainability.

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Send PDF with XML file via email to your business partners
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

The SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing options for Brazil allows you to send DANFE (NF-e) and DACTE (CT-e) in PDF format to your business partners via email. For SAP S/4HANA and SAP ERP, you use the NF-e/CT-e Monitor (J1BNFE) transaction to display the distribution status of the XML and PDF files for each electronic fiscal document.

Take a look at the updates in our What's New session.

Instructions to generate and submit the PDF with the XML file:

1 - Follow the manual instructions in SAP Note 3532295 to customize the attached form template (template.xdp) using the Adobe LiveCycle Designer. The file is a sample that can be used to create the template for the DANFE (NF-e). The maximum template size is set to 2 MB; remember to resize or compress images used in the file.

Editing the sample file in Adobe LiveCycle Designer.

2 - After creating a new template as an XDP file in the Adobe LiveCycle Designer, use a tool to encode the file to Base64 and save it to your computer. Since an XDP file has an XML format, you can copy its source code using a simple text editor before encoding it.

The content of an XDP file opened in a text editor.

3 - You must use an HTTP client or requests via curl to update the template in the service. In the following instructions, an HTTP client is used to create the requests (GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE) and set the endpoint to the URL https://nfe.cfapps.br10.hana.ondemand.com. Refer to the SAP Business Accelerator Hub for further details.

3.1 – The following process relies on token authentication (also known as bearer authentication), which is used over HTTPS (SSL). This authentication allows you to access URLs of our service. The client must send the token in the authorization header when making requests to protected resources:

 

Authorization: Bearer <token>

 

For more details about token authentication in the SAP BTP, check the following pages:

  • OAuth JWT Bearer Authentication
  • OAuth 2.0 – Understanding Token Exchange

3.2 - In the HTTP client, create a POST request with the following details:

  • URL: https://nfe.cfapps.br10.hana.ondemand.com/pdf-service/v1/templates
  • Method: POST
  • Auth: Input the generated authentication token to communicate with our cloud services. Use your clientId and clientSecret to get the token in the SAP BTP.

3.3 – In the Body, you must send the content using the HTTP client, filling in the following details:

  • Template name in templateName field, which can be whatever you want.
  • Electronic document type in documentType field, which must be filled in with “NFE” for DANFE or “CTE” for DACTE.
  • Action type in actionType field, filled in with “AUTHORIZE”.
  • Country in country field, filled in with “BR”.
  • Electronic document layout in the version field; this is an optional field, and in the example, it is filled in with “4.00”, which is the current NF-e (DANFE) layout version.
  • Environment Type in environmentType field; this is an optional field, and it is filled in with “HOMOLOGATION” for testing purposes, or “PRODUCTION”.
  • The content of the XDP template, encoded to Base64, must be pasted into xdpTemplate field.

 

{
	"templateName": "Name of the template",
	"documentType": "NFE",
	"actionType": "AUTHORIZE",
	"country": "BR",
	"version": "4.00",
	"environmentType": "HOMOLOGATION",
	"xdpTemplate":
"Content of the template encoded to Base64 here"
}

 

Template update using an HTTP client.

Note: There is a restriction for documentType, actionType, country, version, and environmentType; you should not send two or more templates that have the same values in these fields.

4 - Open the following pages in SAP Help to understand how to update the email addresses of your business partners and confirm that jobs are scheduled in your backend system.

  • Configuring Email Addresses to Send XML and PDF Files
  • Scheduling Jobs for NF-e, CT-e, NFS-e

5 - Submit an NF-e document to your business partner in the NF-e/CT-e Monitor (J1BNFE) transaction. When the document is authorized by SEFAZ, the Distribution Log column will be updated.

6 - Click on the icon in the Distribution Log column to check the details of the email sent in Distribution History > Log. For more details, check the Distribution Bounce Statuses page in SAP Help.

Steps in the NF-e/CT-e Monitor (J1BNFE) transaction.

The email with the PDF and XML files will be received by your business partner.

Note: After updating the template, it may take up to 15 minutes for the new version to be used, after which your business partners will receive the new PDF version and XML file via email.

You can sign up for email alerts when new SAP Notes are released or updated in the component LOD-LH-NFE (Outbound invoicing option for Brazil); just follow the instructions in SAP Knowledge Base Article 2171560.

Use the regulatory change manager tool to find out if a solution to a legal change is planned. Take a look at SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 and the regulatory change manager page to understand how to use this tool.

Thank you, and until next time,
Thiago


Tags: brasil, einvoice, einvoicing, e-invoicing, electronic invoice, electronic invoicing, electronic document, electronic documents, eletronic document, edocument, edoc, e-document, e-docs, e-doc, e-docs, drc, document compliance, nota fiscal eletrônica, eletronica, electronic nota fiscal, factura electrónica, conhecimento de transporte eletrônico, nf-e, ct-e

entry.title: Credit Management for Advanced Execution Order Item via Adhoc Billing
entry.content:
This blog will help you to understand how Adhoc Billing takes place in advanced execution order item.

In the billing solution adapted for S/4 Hana Service Transactions, known as Service Orders (SO) will be via Billing via Billing Plan After Release (Adhoc Billing). During this process user must create the bill plan.

Once the billing plan is added and service order is released, it will qualify for Credit Commitment update, Hence credit management integration process needs to be triggered, Following are the key categories where credit exposures movement takes place. Transaction code to view Credit Exposures: “UKM_COMMITMENTS”.

Type of Credit Exposures Category

Description of Credit Exposures Category

110

Service Order & Service Contract

410

Service Confirmations

500

Billing Document Value

200

Open Invoices
